title: Cognitive Load Assessment
description: This survey measures the degree to which your mind is cluttered with excessive information and unfinished tasks.

questions.text: How many unfinished tasks are currently occupying your thoughts?

options.label: More than 10, I have lost count

questions.text: When you sit down to focus on one thing, how quickly do unrelated thoughts intrude?

options.label: They rarely intrude; I stay focused
Almost immediately, I cannot stay on track

questions.text: How often do you forget where you placed everyday objects (keys, phone, etc.)?
How would you describe the number of open browser tabs or apps you typically keep running?

options.label: I keep only what I need at the moment
I lose count; dozens are always open

questions.text: How often do you start a new task before finishing the previous one?

options.label: Rarely — I complete tasks one by one
Almost always, I rarely finish anything fully

questions.text: How do you feel when you receive a new notification (message, email, alert)?

options.label: Calm — I check it when convenient
I feel compelled to check it soon
I feel anxious until I check it
I check it immediately no matter what I am doing

questions.text: How well can you recall details from a conversation you had earlier today?

options.label: Very well — I remember most details
Only the main points, details are blurry
Vaguely — I struggle to reconstruct it
Barely at all — I frequently lose track

questions.text: How often do you re-read the same line or paragraph because your mind wandered?
How often do you feel mentally drained even after light activities (browsing, chatting)?
How difficult is it for you to make simple everyday decisions (what to eat, what to watch)?

options.label: I feel paralysed and frequently ask others to decide for me

questions.text: How often do you feel the urge to check social media or news even when you planned not to?
When you try to relax, how successfully can you mentally switch off from work or responsibilities?

options.label: I cannot switch off at all

questions.text: How often do you write something down (note, reminder) only to forget what you wrote and why?

options.label: Almost always — my notes feel chaotic

questions.text: How often do you find yourself in a room and unable to remember why you went there?
How would you rate the overall clarity of your mind at the end of a typical day?

options.label: Very clear — I feel refreshed and organized
Mostly clear with slight mental tiredness
Completely cluttered — I feel mentally exhausted

questions.text: How often do you feel that the amount of information you consume daily is too much to process?
How well do you prioritise tasks when multiple things demand your attention simultaneously?

options.label: Very well — I create a clear order
I find it very difficult and often freeze
I cannot prioritise — everything feels equally urgent

questions.text: How often do you experience a mental 'blank' mid-sentence, forgetting the word or idea you were about to say?
How does your mental state feel after spending an hour scrolling through online content?

options.label: Drained, overwhelmed, and unable to focus

questions.text: How often do you feel that your brain has too many 'tabs open' — thoughts competing for attention?

options.label: Almost never — my mind feels orderly

scoring.description: Your mind is clear and well-organised. You process information efficiently and maintain excellent mental clarity. No intervention needed.
You experience mild mental clutter. Occasional digital detox and task-list habits will keep you at your best.
Noticeable mental clutter is affecting your focus. Consider structured note-taking, priority lists, and regular information breaks.
Your brain is significantly overloaded. Deliberate digital minimalism, mindfulness practices, and workload restructuring are strongly advised.
You are carrying an excessive cognitive burden. Professional guidance in stress and information management is recommended.
Your cognitive resources are critically depleted. Immediate lifestyle changes and professional psychological support are urgently needed.
You are in a state of cognitive crisis. Please seek immediate support from a mental health professional or counsellor.
